description_long: This object is a medium-sized, fixed-wing airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a slender fuselage. The wings are long and straight, extending symmetrically from the central fuselage, and feature no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The airplane is equipped with two engines mounted under the wings, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a nose wheel, all of which are extended and appear fixed. The tail section features a T-tail design, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a streamlined nose section. The exterior is primarily a light gray color, giving it a utilitarian and modern appearance.
description: A light gray, high-wing airplane with a T-tail, twin underwing engines, and fixed tricycle landing gear. The design is streamlined and modern, with an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the straight, high-mounted wings, T-tail, and overall symmetrical layout.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the slender fuselage, underwing engines, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the landing gear arrangement, engine placement, and underside of the wings and fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the underwing engines, landing gear, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin underwing engines, likely turboprop or jet based on nacelle shape.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed and non-retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit at the nose of the fuselage, likely single or dual seat.
